data.title: Novartis gets EU positive recommendation for its kidney disease treatment
data.description:
Novartis (NYSE:NVS) announced Friday that the European Medicines Agency (EMA), endorsed Fabhalta for the treatment of adults with C3 glomerulopathy.

Issuing a positive opinion, EMA’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) has recommended the marketing authorization for Fabhalta to treat C3 glomerulopathy, a rare kidney disease.The CHMP recommendation will next be reviewed by the European Commission (EC) before a final decision on marketing authorization which is expected within two months.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Friday: FUBO, ADSK, META and LLY
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures were largely higher on Friday, as market participants received a key inflation reading that came in-line with expectations. Here are some stocks to watch on Friday:

  • Shares of FuboTV (NYSE:FUBO) gave up earlier gains to slip nearly 9% in pre-market trading. The live sports streaming platform delivered a mixed Q4 report, missing on the top-line but beating bottom-line expectations. FUBO said Q4 marked its first quarter of positive free cash flow. The company ended full-year 2024 with 1.676M paid subscribers in North America. For Q1 2025, FuboTV (NYSE:FUBO) sees North American revenue of $400M to $410M and subscribers of 1.43M to 1.46M. FUBO in January said Disney's (DIS) Hulu + Live TV business will merge with Fubo.
  • Autodesk (NASDAQ:ADSK) stock added about 1% ahead of the opening bell, after the software firm said it would lay off 1,350 employees as part of a worldwide restructuring plan. It also delivered a FQ4 2025 top- and bottom-line beat, with total quarterly billings jumping 23% to $2.11B. Autodesk (NASDAQ:ADSK) software is used in the construction industry, by design engineers and by media professionals for animation and visual effects. Top boss Andrew Anagnost on the earnings conference call said the restructuring plan will enable ADSK "to deliver GAAP margins among the best in the industry."
  • Class A shares of Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) were in focus, up slightly ahead of market open. A CNBC report said the Facebook-parent intended to release its own artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot app in an effort to compete with the likes of OpenAI's ChatGPT and Google's Gemini. The app is expected to debut in the second quarter, CNBC said, citing people familiar with the matter. Meta (NASDAQ:META) has earlier announced plans to invest as much as $65B on AI projects in 2025, and this move would be more evidence of its AI ambitions.
  • Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) stock was down marginally in pre-market trading, but for the week it was up 3.6%. It has been a busy few days for the world's largest pharmaceutical firm. The company on Tuesday announced the launch of additional vial doses of its blockbuster obesity drug Zepbound. On the same day, it separately said it would acquire biotech Organovo's (ONVO) investigational ulcerative colitis drug. Then on Wednesday, Lilly (NYSE:LLY) unveiled plans to build four new pharmaceutical manufacturing sites in the U.S., bringing its total investment in the country to more than $50B since 2020.

data.title: NuRAN receives second drawdown of $1.05M from Cygnum Capital
data.description:
• NuRAN Wireless (OTCQB:NRRWF) has received approval for the second drawdown of $1.05M from the Facility for Energy Inclusion, a fund managed by Cygnum Capital.
  • This follows the first drawdown of $2.5M received on July 16, 2024, as part of the $5M loan facility agreement announced on April 26, 2024.
  • NuRAN has chosen to dedicate more resources to the region, reinforcing its commitment to accelerate deployments and strengthen its financial performance under the objective of achieveing EBITDA positive as soon as possible.
  • "The receipt of this second drawdown is a testament to the strong partnership between NuRAN and FEI as we continue our mission to expand mobile connectivity in remote areas across Africa. These funds will help us achieve further significant progress towards our 2025 objectives." said Francis Létourneau, CEO of NuRAN Wireless.

data.title: Pacific Basin Shipping GAAP EPS of $0.199, revenue of $2.58B
data.description:

data.title: Autodesk, Elastic, DoubleVerify in focus amid flurry of earnings from enterprise software
data.description:
• Enterprise software stocks were in focus on Friday amid a flurry of earnings from the sector, including from industry heavyweights such as Autodesk (NASDAQ:ADSK), Elastic (ESTC) and DoubleVerify (NYSE:DV).
  • Autodesk shares rose 1.5% in premarket trading, as the construction software firm reported fourth-quarter results that topped estimates and said it was laying off 9% of its workforce, amid pressure from an activist investor. Autodesk also said that Mary T. McDowell would step down from its board of directors.
  • Elastic shares jumped 12.5% after the enterprise search company reported third-quarter results that beat Wall Street's estimates and issued a stronger-than-expected forecast for the coming quarter. Following the results, investment firm Scotiabank raised its price target on Elastic to $140 from $127.
  • Expensify (EXFY) jumped 15% after the expense software firm reported better-than-expected fourth-quarter results and issued an outlook for 2025.
  • SoundHound AI (NASDAQ:SOUN) shares rose 2.5% in premarket trading after the voice assistant software company reported fourth-quarter results that topped estimates and upped its outlook for 2025.
  • Alkami Technology (OTC:ALKM) shares were little changed after the financial software firm reported results that were mostly in-line with estimates.
  • On the flip side, DoubleVerify tumbled 20% as its fourth-quarter results and outlook for fiscal 2025 fell short of Wall Street's expectations.
  • Palantir (PLTR) shares fell 5.5% in premarket trading, continuing their string of losses, after the company named Jeffrey Buckley as its Chief Accounting Officer.
  • Jamf Holdings (JAMF) shares fell 4.5% after the company reported fourth-quarter results.

data.title: Grupo Simec, S.A.B. de C.V. reports Q4 results
